rationale.R1.1: A general request to look at the paragraph centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the paragraph's argumentative progression from prior work to method, results, and contribution is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the paragraph makes quantitative empirical claims, reviewing whether reported results support the stated conclusions is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A broad paragraph-level review naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague review request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth of review.
rationale.RX.2: The paragraph contains clinical and first-work claims, so avoiding inflated conclusions is an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection of responsibility and not actually critique, rewrite, or verify the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns clinical deployment and triage, downstream-harm avoidance is active in interpreting review responsibilities.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
